search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae abdominal bloating primary care guideline UAE
site:nice.org.uk CG61 bloating distension bowel habit red flags investigations follow-up
site:nice.org.uk NG12 persistent abdominal distension bloating ovarian colorectal
candidate_sources_rejected: DHA abdominal-pain telehealth guidance used as complete evidence for isolated bloating
IBS management recommendations used to diagnose or treat undifferentiated bloating
ovarian-cancer symptom guidance applied without age, anatomy, persistence, or frequency qualifiers
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Pain guidance does not cover isolated or chronic bloating as a complete presentation.
The workflow does not establish the adult duration or diagnostic criteria for IBS.
Cancer guidance is applicable only in recommendation-qualified contexts and cannot define the generic workflow.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Adults with recurrent abdominal bloating where the exact IBS symptom domains are relevant; acute illness, children, pregnancy, postoperative, pelvic, inflammatory, obstructive, malignant, and systemic contexts require separate evidence.
setting_applicability: Primary-care documentation and follow-up as qualified by the exact source and clinical context.
UAE_applicability: International evidence requires UAE primary-care, laboratory, prescribing, referral, safeguarding, and local-pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Exact official documents and sections were reviewed on 2026-07-14.
superseded_check: The cited exact source remains current on the issuing organisation website at the review date.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact adult IBS symptom-profile section supports documenting bloating, distension, meal association, bowel-pattern change, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, mucus, duration, and impact without assigning IBS or another cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports at least 6 months of abdominal pain or discomfort, bloating, bowel-habit change, relation to defaecation or stool frequency or form, straining, urgency, incomplete evacuation, food association, mucus, and selected extra-intestinal symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports clinician-assessed abdominal, systemic, inflammatory, and cancer-concern documentation without asserting that examination occurred or that concern is absent.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Requires clinical assessment and examination for suspected-cancer features and inflammatory markers for inflammatory bowel disease, with referral when present; specific cancer-feature fields require their own exact source.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact diagnostic-test section supports recording already reviewed blood and coeliac-test context only when the adult IBS criteria and clinician context apply; it does not order tests.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports full blood count, ESR or plasma viscosity, CRP, and coeliac antibodies in people meeting IBS criteria and identifies tests not required solely to confirm IBS.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact follow-up section supports clinician-entered follow-up, red-flag reassessment, and patient discussion without generating dietary, medicine, investigation, or referral advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports agreed symptom-response-based follow-up, annual review, and investigation or referral when new red-flag symptoms emerge.

unresolved_source_gaps: Acute onset, pregnancy, pelvic symptoms, visible distension, examination, ovarian context, obstruction, ascites, medicine, diet, exact tests, diagnosis, referral, urgency, and interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
